source_label: AP Physics 1 — Unit 3 Work, Energy, Power (workbook MCQ, vision-extracted)
_notes: Hand-curated from workbook MCQ pages 117-122 + answer key pages 152-154 via Claude vision read. 39 source MCQs minus Q27 (pure dynamics — vector addition of impulses) and Q36 (constant-velocity net force, pure Unit 2) = 37 imports across 14 new Unit-3 QTs.

question_types.label: Work done by a constant force at an angle (W = Fd cos θ)

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A 2 kg ball is attached to a 0.80 m string and whirled in a horizontal circle at a constant speed of 6 m/s. The work done on the ball during each revolution is:

(A) 90 J
(B) 72 J
(C) 16 J
(D) zero

source_reference.worksheet_name: Barron's AP Physics 1 Premium 2024 (Rideout & Wolf)

ordered_content.value: A box is pulled along a smooth floor by a force F, making an angle θ with the horizontal. As θ increases, the amount of work done to pull the box the same distance, d,

(A) increases
(B) increases and then decreases
(C) remains the same
(D) decreases

source_reference.worksheet_name: Barron's AP Physics 1 Premium 2024 (Rideout & Wolf)

ordered_content.value: When you hold up a 10-kg mass with your arms outstretched, you get tired. However, according to physics, you have not done any work! Explain how this is possible.

question_types.label: Work done by friction (sign and magnitude)

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A force F at an angle θ above the horizontal is used to pull a heavy suitcase of weight mg a distance d along a level floor at constant velocity. The coefficient of friction between the floor and the suitcase is μ. The work done by the frictional force is:

(A) –Fd cos θ
(B) –μFd cos θ
(C) –μmgd
(D) –μmgd cos θ

question_types.label: Work done by a varying force (area under F-x graph)

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: The following graphs, all drawn to the same scale, represent the net force F as a function of displacement x for an object that moves along a straight line. Which graph represents the force that will cause the greatest change in the kinetic energy of the object from x = 0 to x = x₁?

(A) graph A
(B) graph B
(C) graph C
(D) graph D

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A softball player catches a ball of mass m, which is moving towards her with horizontal speed V. While bringing the ball to rest, her hand moved back a distance d. Assuming constant deceleration, the horizontal force exerted on the ball by the hand is

(A) mV²/(2d)
(B) mV²/d
(C) 2mV/d
(D) mV/d

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A rock is lifted for a certain time by a force F that is greater in magnitude than the rock's weight W. The change in kinetic energy of the rock during this time is equal to the

(A) work done by the net force (F − W)
(B) work done by F alone
(C) work done by W alone
(D) difference in the potential energy of the rock before and after this time.

options: (A) work done by the net force (F − W)
(D) difference in the potential energy of the rock before and after this time.
questions.correct_option: (A) work done by the net force (F − W)

question_types.label: Kinetic energy and speed (KE = ½mv², ratios and scaling)

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A rock is dropped from the top of a tall tower. Half a second later another rock, twice as massive as the first, is dropped. Ignoring air resistance,

(A) the distance between the rocks increases while both are falling.
(B) the acceleration is greater for the more massive rock.
(C) they strike the ground more than half a second apart.
(D) they strike the ground with the same kinetic energy.

options: (A) the distance between the rocks increases while both are falling.
(B) the acceleration is greater for the more massive rock.
(C) they strike the ground more than half a second apart.
(D) they strike the ground with the same kinetic energy.
questions.correct_option: (A) the distance between the rocks increases while both are falling.

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: What is the kinetic energy of a satellite of mass m that orbits the Earth, of mass M, in a circular orbit of radius R?

(A) ½ GMm/R
(B) ¼ GMm/R
(C) ½ GMm/R²
(D) GMm/R²

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: An object initially at rest is acted on by a constant force in the +x direction for 1 s, then no force for 1 s, then a constant force of the same magnitude in the +y direction for 1 s. Which of the following graphs best represents the kinetic energy K of the object as a function of time?

(A) graph A
(B) graph B
(C) graph C
(D) graph D

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: An object is dropped from rest from a certain height. Air resistance is negligible. After falling a distance d, the object's kinetic energy is proportional to which of the following?

(A) 1/d²
(B) 1/d
(C) √d
(D) d

source_reference.worksheet_name: Barron's AP Physics 1 Premium 2024 (Rideout & Wolf)

ordered_content.value: Two unequal masses are dropped simultaneously from the same height. The two masses will experience the same change in

(A) acceleration
(B) kinetic energy
(C) potential energy
(D) velocity

source_reference.worksheet_name: Barron's AP Physics 1 Premium 2024 (Rideout & Wolf)

ordered_content.value: Explain how it might be possible for a moving object to possess, and simultaneously not possess, kinetic energy?

question_types.label: Gravitational potential energy near Earth (ΔU = mgh)

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A pendulum bob of mass m on a cord of length L is pulled sideways until the cord makes an angle θ with the vertical. The change in (gravitational) potential energy of the bob during the displacement is:

(A) mgL(1 − cos θ)
(B) mgL(1 − sin θ)
(C) mgL sin θ
(D) mgL cos θ

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A system consists of two objects having masses m₁ and m₂ (m₁ < m₂). The objects are connected by a massless string, hung over a pulley as shown, and then released. When the object of mass m₂ has descended a distance h, the potential energy of the system has decreased by

(A) (m₂ − m₁)gh
(B) m₂gh
(C) (m₁ + m₂)gh
(D) ½(m₁ + m₂)gh

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A block of mass 3.0 kg is hung from a spring, causing it to stretch 12 cm at equilibrium. The 3.0 kg block is then replaced by a 4.0 kg block, and the new block is released from the position shown, at which the spring is unstretched. How far will the 4.0 kg block fall before its direction is reversed?

(A) 18 cm
(B) 24 cm
(C) 32 cm
(D) 48 cm

source_reference.worksheet_name: Barron's AP Physics 1 Premium 2024 (Rideout & Wolf)

ordered_content.value: Which of the following are the units for the spring constant, k?

(A) kg · m²/s²
(B) kg · s²
(C) kg · m/s
(D) kg/s²

source_reference.worksheet_name: Barron's AP Physics 1 Premium 2024 (Rideout & Wolf)

ordered_content.value: A 0.3-kg mass rests on top of a spring that has been compressed by 0.04 m. Neglect any frictional effects, and consider the spring to be massless. Then, if the spring has a constant k equal to 2,000 N/m, to what height will the mass rise when the system is released?

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 2014 Released Exam

ordered_content.value: (12 points, suggested 25 min) A student hangs a 0.125-kg object on a spring, sets it into oscillation, and obtains data for the position and velocity of the object as a function of time (graphs given). (a) On the grid below, sketch the potential energy of the object-spring-Earth system as a function of time. Explicitly label any intercepts, asymptotes, maxima, or minima with numerical values, as appropriate. (b) On the axes below, sketch the acceleration of the object as a function of time. Explicitly label any intercepts, asymptotes, maxima, or minima with numerical values, as appropriate. Next the student is given a rubber band and asked to determine whether the relationship between the restoring force exerted by the rubber band and the amount it is stretched is the same as that of an ideal spring. (c) Describe an experimental procedure that the student could use to collect the necessary data, including all the equipment the student would need. (d) How should the student analyze the data to determine whether the relationship between the restoring force exerted by the rubber band and the amount it is stretched is the same as that of an ideal spring? What evidence from the analysis would be used to make the determination?

question_types.label: Conservation of mechanical energy (KE + PE = const, no friction)

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A pendulum is pulled to one side and released. It swings freely to the opposite side and stops. Which of the following might best represent graphs of kinetic energy (E_k), potential energy (E_p), and total mechanical energy (E_T)?

(A) graph A
(B) graph B
(C) graph C
(D) graph D

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A rock of mass m is thrown horizontally off a building from a height h. The speed of the rock as it leaves the thrower's hand at the edge of the building is v₀. What is the kinetic energy of the rock just before it hits the ground?

(A) mgh
(B) ½mv₀²
(C) ½mv₀² · mgh
(D) ½mv₀² + mgh

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 2016 Released Exam

ordered_content.value: (7 points, suggested 13 min) Two blocks A and B of equal mass are on a frictionless track. Block A, initially moving with speed v_1, has a perfectly elastic collision with block B. Block B has a speed v_1 immediately after the collision, and then it travels around a vertical loop of radius R, where R is much larger than the size of the blocks. The speed of block B at the top of the loop is v_top. Block B then slides up a ramp until it comes momentarily to rest at a height h above the floor. (a) Derive an equation for the height h in terms of m, R, v_top, and g, as appropriate.

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A car of mass m slides across a patch of ice at a speed v with its brakes locked. It then hits dry pavement and skids to a stop in a distance d. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the tires and the dry road is μ. If the car has a mass of 2m, it would have skidded a distance of

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A car of mass m slides across a patch of ice at a speed v with its brakes locked. It then hits dry pavement and skids to a stop in a distance d. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the tires and the dry road is μ. If the car has a speed of 2v, it would have skidded a distance of

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A fan blows the air and gives it kinetic energy. An hour after the fan has been turned off, what has happened to the kinetic energy of the air?

(A) it disappears
(B) it turns into potential energy
(C) it turns into thermal energy
(D) it turns into sound energy

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A rough semicircular track has its ends at a vertical height h. A block placed at point P at one end of the track is released from rest and slides past the bottom of the track. Which of the following is true of the height to which the block rises on the other side of the track?

(A) It is equal to h/4
(B) It is equal to h/2
(C) It is equal to h
(D) It is between zero and h; the exact height depends on how much energy is lost to friction.

options: (D) It is between zero and h; the exact height depends on how much energy is lost to friction.
questions.correct_option: (D) It is between zero and h; the exact height depends on how much energy is lost to friction.

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A block of mass M on a horizontal surface is connected to the end of a massless spring of spring constant k. The block is pulled a distance x from equilibrium and rests there. The block is then released and moves toward equilibrium. What coefficient of kinetic friction between the surface and the block would allow the block to return to equilibrium and stop?

(A) kx²/(2Mg)
(B) kx/(Mg)
(C) kx/(2Mg)
(D) Mg/(2kx)

source_reference.worksheet_name: Barron's AP Physics 1 Premium 2024 (Rideout & Wolf)

ordered_content.value: A mass m is moving horizontally along a nearly frictionless floor with velocity v. The mass now encounters a part of the floor that has a coefficient of kinetic friction given by µ. The total distance traveled by the mass before it is slowed by friction to a stop is given by

(A) 2v²/(µg)
(B) v²/(2µg)
(C) 2mgv²
(D) mv²/(2g)

source_reference.worksheet_name: Barron's AP Physics 1 Premium 2024 (Rideout & Wolf)

ordered_content.value: A 0.75-kg sphere is dropped through a tall column of liquid. When the sphere has fallen a distance of 2.0 m, it is observed to have a velocity of 5 m/s. (a) How much work was done by the frictional 'viscosity' of the liquid? (b) What is the average force of friction during the displacement of 2.0 m?

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 2015 Released Exam

ordered_content.value: (7 points, suggested 13 min) Cart A has mass M and is released from rest at a height 2H on a ramp making angle 2θ with the horizontal. Cart B has mass 2M and is released from rest at a height H on a ramp making angle θ with the horizontal. The carts roll toward each other, have a head-on collision on the horizontal portion of the ramp, and stick together. The masses of the carts' wheels are negligible, as are any frictional or drag forces. (a) Indicate whether the carts remain at rest, move to the left, or move to the right after the collision (Remain at rest / Move to the left / Move to the right). Explain how you arrived at your answer. (b) Consider the time interval from when the two carts are released until just after they collide.   (i) For the system consisting of the two carts and Earth, indicate whether the total mechanical energy increases, decreases, or remains the same. Justify your answer.   (ii) For the system consisting of only the two carts, indicate whether the total mechanical energy increases, decreases, or remains the same. Justify your answer.

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A mass m attached to a horizontal massless spring with spring constant k is set into simple harmonic motion. Its maximum displacement from its equilibrium position is A. What is the mass's speed as it passes through its equilibrium position?

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A mass m is attached to a spring with a spring constant k. If the mass is set into motion by a displacement d from its equilibrium position, what would be the speed v of the mass when it returns to the equilibrium position?

(A) v = √(kd/m)
(B) v = d√(k/m)
(C) v = √(kd/(mg))
(D) v² = mgd/k

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: Which of the following is true for a system consisting of a mass oscillating on the end of an ideal spring?

(A) The kinetic and potential energies are equal to each other at all times.
(B) The maximum potential energy is achieved when the mass passes through its equilibrium position.
(C) The maximum kinetic energy and maximum potential energy are equal, but occur at different times.
(D) The maximum kinetic energy occurs at maximum displacement of the mass from its equilibrium position.

options: (A) The kinetic and potential energies are equal to each other at all times.
(B) The maximum potential energy is achieved when the mass passes through its equilibrium position.
(C) The maximum kinetic energy and maximum potential energy are equal, but occur at different times.
(D) The maximum kinetic energy occurs at maximum displacement of the mass from its equilibrium position.
questions.correct_option: (C) The maximum kinetic energy and maximum potential energy are equal, but occur at different times.

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A sphere of mass m₁, attached to a spring, is displaced downward from equilibrium and released from rest. A sphere of mass m₂, suspended from a string of length L, is displaced to the right and released from rest so that it swings as a pendulum with small amplitude. Assume both spheres undergo simple harmonic motion. Which of the following is true for both spheres?

(A) The maximum kinetic energy is attained as the sphere passes through its equilibrium position.
(B) The maximum gravitational potential energy is attained as the sphere passes through its equilibrium position.
(C) The maximum gravitational potential energy is attained when the sphere reaches its point of release.
(D) The maximum total energy is attained only as the sphere passes through its equilibrium position.

options: (A) The maximum kinetic energy is attained as the sphere passes through its equilibrium position.
(B) The maximum gravitational potential energy is attained as the sphere passes through its equilibrium position.
(C) The maximum gravitational potential energy is attained when the sphere reaches its point of release.
(D) The maximum total energy is attained only as the sphere passes through its equilibrium position.
questions.correct_option: (A) The maximum kinetic energy is attained as the sphere passes through its equilibrium position.

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A block on a horizontal frictionless plane is attached to a spring. The block oscillates along the x-axis with amplitude A. Which of the following statements about energy is correct?

(A) The potential energy of the spring is at a minimum at x = 0.
(B) The potential energy of the spring is at a minimum at x = A.
(C) The kinetic energy of the block is at a minimum at x = 0.
(D) The kinetic energy of the block is at a maximum at x = A.

options: (A) The potential energy of the spring is at a minimum at x = 0.
(B) The potential energy of the spring is at a minimum at x = A.
(C) The kinetic energy of the block is at a minimum at x = 0.
(D) The kinetic energy of the block is at a maximum at x = A.
questions.correct_option: (A) The potential energy of the spring is at a minimum at x = 0.

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: An ideal massless spring is fixed to the wall at one end. A block of mass M attached to the other end of the spring oscillates with amplitude A on a frictionless horizontal surface. The maximum speed of the block is v_m. The force constant of the spring is

(A) Mgv_m/(2A)
(B) Mv_m²/(2A)
(C) Mv_m²/A²
(D) Mv_m²/(2A²)

source_reference.worksheet_name: Barron's AP Physics 1 Premium 2024 (Rideout & Wolf)

ordered_content.value: A 15-kg mass is attached to a massless spring by a light string that passes over a frictionless pulley. The spring has a force constant of 500 N/m and is unstretched when the mass is released. What is the velocity of the mass when it has fallen a distance of 0.3 m?

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A ball swings freely back and forth in an arc from point I to point IV. Point II is the lowest point in the path, III is located 0.5 m above II, and IV is 1 m above II. Air resistance is negligible. If the potential energy is zero at point II, where will the kinetic and potential energies of the ball be equal?

(A) At point II
(B) At some point between II and III
(C) At point III
(D) At some point between III and IV

options: (B) At some point between II and III
(D) At some point between III and IV

(A) √(gL)
(B) √(2gL)

(D) gL

(A) √(2gℓ(1 − cos θ))
(B) √(2gℓ cos θ)
(C) √(2gℓ sin θ)
(D) 2gℓ(1 − cos θ)

question_types.label: Power: P = W/t and P = Fv

ordered_content.value: A child pushes horizontally on a box of mass m which moves with constant speed v across a horizontal floor. The coefficient of friction between the box and floor is μ. At what rate does the child do work on the box?

(C) μmg/v
(D) mg/v

(A) Ft/m

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A football is kicked off the ground a distance of 50 yards downfield. Neglecting air resistance, which of the following statements would be INCORRECT when the football reaches the highest point?

(A) all of the ball's original kinetic energy has been changed into potential energy
(B) the ball's horizontal velocity is the same as when it left the kicker's foot
(C) the ball will have been in the air one-half of its total flight time
(D) the vertical component of the velocity is equal to zero

options: (A) all of the ball's original kinetic energy has been changed into potential energy
(B) the ball's horizontal velocity is the same as when it left the kicker's foot
(C) the ball will have been in the air one-half of its total flight time
(D) the vertical component of the velocity is equal to zero
questions.correct_option: (A) all of the ball's original kinetic energy has been changed into potential energy

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: From the top of a high cliff, a ball is thrown horizontally with initial speed v₀. Which of the following graphs best represents the ball's kinetic energy K as a function of time t?

(A) graph A
(B) graph B
(C) graph C
(D) graph D

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: A spring-loaded gun can fire a projectile to a height h if it is fired straight up. If the same gun is pointed at an angle of 45° from the vertical, what maximum height can now be reached by the projectile?

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 Practice Workbook — Book 1 (Mechanics, Waves, Electrostatics) — 2014 compilation

ordered_content.value: An object is projected vertically upward from ground level. It rises to a maximum height H. If air resistance is negligible, which of the following must be true for the object when it is at a height H/2?

(A) Its speed is half of its initial speed.
(B) Its kinetic energy is half of its initial kinetic energy.
(C) Its potential energy is half of its initial potential energy.
(D) Its total mechanical energy is half of its initial value.

options: (A) Its speed is half of its initial speed.
(B) Its kinetic energy is half of its initial kinetic energy.
(C) Its potential energy is half of its initial potential energy.
(D) Its total mechanical energy is half of its initial value.
questions.correct_option: (B) Its kinetic energy is half of its initial kinetic energy.

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 2015 Released Exam

ordered_content.value: (7 points, suggested 13 min) A toy car coasts along the curved track shown above. The car has initial speed v_A when it is at point A at the top of the track, and the car leaves the track at point B with speed v_B at an angle θ above the horizontal. Assume that the rotational kinetic energy of the car's wheels and energy losses due to friction are both negligible. (a) Suppose the toy car is released from rest at point A (v_A = 0).   (i) After the car leaves the track and reaches the highest point in its trajectory it will be at a different height than it was at point A. Briefly explain why this is so.   (ii) Determine the speed of the car when it is at the highest point in its trajectory after leaving the track, in terms of v_B and θ. Briefly explain how you arrived at your answer. (b) Suppose the toy car is given an initial push so that it has nonzero speed at point A. Determine the speed v_A of the car at point A such that the highest point in its trajectory after leaving the track is the same as its height at point A. Express your answer in terms of v_B and θ. Explain how you arrived at your answer.

source_reference.worksheet_name: Barron's AP Physics 1 Premium 2024 (Rideout & Wolf)

ordered_content.value: A 1.5-kg block is placed on an incline. The mass is connected to a massless spring by means of a light string passed over a frictionless pulley. The spring has a force constant k equal to 100 N/m. The block is released from rest, and the spring is initially unstretched. The block moves down a distance of 16 cm before coming to rest. What is the coefficient of kinetic friction between the block and the surface of the incline?

source_reference.worksheet_name: AP Physics 1 2014 Released Exam

ordered_content.value: (7 points, suggested 13 min) The figure above shows part of a system consisting of a block at the top of an inclined plane that rests on a table, which is located on Earth. The block and plane are at rest when the block is released. In trial 1 there is no friction between the block and the plane or between the plane and the table. In trial 2 the plane is fixed to the table so it cannot move, but there is still no friction between the block and the plane. Indicate whether the speed of the block relative to the table when the block reaches the bottom of the plane is greater in trial 1 or trial 2. Justify your answer in a clear, coherent, paragraph-length explanation.
